figure.title: UALink TL Receive Path Block Diagram
figure.purpose: Details the internal components and data flow for the receive (Rx) path within the UALink Transaction Layer.

technical_summary: The flit is first processed by the TL Flit Decoder.
Decoded components are distributed to the Rx Req Queue, Rx Address Cache, Rx Rsp Queue, and the Data/BE/Operand Buffer.
Outputs from these internal structures are forwarded to the external UPLI Originator and Completer.
The Rx Address Cache works with the decoder to expand compressed addresses.

components.name: TL Flit Decoder
components.description: Parses the incoming 64-byte flit into appropriate queues and buffers.

components.name: Rx Address Cache
components.description: Used to reconstruct full addresses from cached/compressed incoming addresses.

ordered_flow: Inbound Flit -> TL Flit Decoder -> Queues/Buffers -> UPLI

critical_facts: The TL receive path relies on a central Flit Decoder to unpack the 64-byte flit and distribute requests, responses, and data to respective queues and external upper layers.
